title: European experts call for the Partisan Memorial Cemetery to be restored for the benefit of all citizens of Mostar, Bosnia and Herzegovina and Europe as a whole
content:
Press release in English | Bosnian | Croatian | Serbian

Europa Nostra, the leading European heritage civil society network, has released today a Report calling for the Partisan Memorial Cemetery in Mostar, Bosnia and Herzegovina, an exceptional work of memorial architecture and landscape design by the renowned Yugoslav architect Bogdan Bogdanović, to be restored, properly managed and made publicly accessible. “This goal can be achieved, with the combined efforts of all levels of governance and with the support of the European Union Institutions and/or Member States,” states the report, which was prepared by a European team of experts in framework of the 7 Most Endangered Programme, run by Europa Nostra with the support of the European Investment Bank Institute and the Creative Europe Programme of the European Union.
